title: plantago durvillei subsp. mollior
intro: Plantago durvillei subsp. mollior, a subspecies of the common plantain, is a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and mucous membranes. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Plantago durvillei subsp. mollior can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
